posture: External references are support only; local Phase 6 and Phase 0 blueprints remain authoritative.

borrowed: retain trace evidence for failure or retry-oriented debugging
trace viewer attaches actions, screenshots, DOM snapshots, console, network, and metadata for review
applied: Phase 6 browser proof keeps deterministic screenshots, aria snapshots, and failure-artifact posture instead of green screenshots alone
rejected: always-on trace capture for every passing gate run because it is heavier than needed for PHI-safe local proof

borrowed: browser contexts provide isolated clean-slate runs
applied: 369 to 371 proof is interpreted as reliable because patient and staff surfaces use isolated contexts
rejected: shared browser state as release evidence

borrowed: automated accessibility checks detect only part of accessibility risk
applied: 371 evidence combines DOM semantics, keyboard flows, aria snapshots, reflow, reduced motion, and visual baselines
rejected: treating automated checks as a replacement for manual assistive technology assessment

borrowed: visual comparisons require repeatable screenshot baselines
applied: 371 visual baselines supplement but do not replace semantic proof
rejected: visual screenshots as the sole release gate signal

borrowed: Pharmacy First operational interpretation must remain tied to service specification and clinical pathway updates
applied: policy-pack versioning, patient-choice truth, and urgent-return separation remain launch-critical
rejected: hard-coding Pharmacy First clinical pathway rules outside versioned policy packs

borrowed: DCB0129 and DCB0160 compliance is mandatory under the Health and Social Care Act 2012
applied: SCAL and clinical-safety sign-off are carry-forward launch constraints rather than local proof claims
rejected: declaring live clinical-safety approval from repository-only test evidence

borrowed: manufacturers must evidence clinical risk management for health IT systems
applied: hazard log and clinical safety case are carried into Phase 7 launch conditions
rejected: using a local hazard coverage matrix as a substitute for a signed clinical safety case

borrowed: NHS App web integration uses Sandpit and AOS stages, SCAL, connection agreement, limited release, incident rehearsal, and post-live reporting
applied: Phase 7 is allowed to start as constrained preparation, not as NHS App go-live
rejected: treating Phase 6 completion as NHS App release approval

borrowed: NHS App integrations must meet WCAG 2.2 AA, the NHS service standard, clinical safety standards, and data privacy standards
applied: manual accessibility and SCAL constraints remain explicit carry-forward entries
rejected: claiming NHS App readiness from local Playwright proof alone

borrowed: NHS accessibility testing combines automated testing, manual testing, keyboard checks, and zoom or reflow checks
applied: 371 proof is accepted for local gate, while manual assistive testing remains carry-forward
rejected: waiving manual checks because browser automation passed

borrowed: 320 CSS pixel equivalent reflow is a release-relevant accessibility check
applied: 371 reflow proof is a critical evidence reference
rejected: approving layouts that require page-level horizontal scrolling at 320 CSS pixels
